verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family, but unlike IZUMO1 (sperm-specific surface protein) or IZUMO1R/JUNO (GPI-anchored egg surface protein), IZUMO4 lacks a classical TM domain and is annotated as nuclear by NCBI/Alliance of Genome Resources. UniProt Q1ZYL8 shows no signal peptide or TM helix. Pre-no checklist: no documented cancer/cell-death ecto-pool; no evidence of germline/gamete-restricted PM display despite sperm-related family members (IZUMO4 is nuclear-localized, not sperm-surface like IZUMO1); not a lysosomal TM protein; no documented PM minority pool or secretory cycling; no wash-resistant TM-partner tethering reported. No surface-directed therapeutic programs found. No surface biotinylation or flow-cytometry evidence in the literature for this protein body. IZUMO family membership alone does not confer surface biology when gene-specific localization is nuclear.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO4 is poorly characterized; nuclear annotation may be incomplete and no surface proteomics data were found to conclusively rule out a minor PM pool.
